fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
India won its first Cricket World Cup in 1983 at Lord's, defeating the two-time defending champions West Indies by 43 runs.
The Indian Premier League (IPL), founded in 2008, is the most-attended cricket league in the world and one of the highest-paid sports leagues per player.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Lionel Messi won his first FIFA World Cup title with Argentina in 2022, defeating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition, with Real Madrid holding the record for most titles.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
PV Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton at the 2016 Rio Olympics.
The BWF World Championships is held annually and is one of the most prestigious individual events in badminton.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and the US Open.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most men's Grand Slam singles titles in tennis history.
Wimbledon, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam still played on grass.
